    "R1.3": "Quantitative claims (4.2% / 6.7% / 40× / ablation delta) must be traced to the described experimental setup, and the headline claim that results 'demonstrate the value of embedding physical priors' must be checked against the actual evidence (data-driven MSE training, no PDE residual loss reported)."

rationale.R1.1: The section makes explicit contribution claims (SpectralPINN, spectral activations, gap vs mesh-based solvers) that a review is expected to interrogate for clarity, accuracy, and positioning.
rationale.R1.2: Multiple logical tensions require checking: the method is trained purely on MSE against DNS snapshots yet is labeled 'physics-informed'; the 'spectral approach' framing is attributed to sinusoidal activations rather than any spectral decomposition; the concluding generalization ('physical priors') is unsupported by the described training objective.
rationale.R1.3: Quantitative claims (4.2% / 6.7% / 40× / ablation delta) must be traced to the described experimental setup, and the headline claim that results 'demonstrate the value of embedding physical priors' must be checked against the actual evidence (data-driven MSE training, no PDE residual loss reported).

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review prioritize technical soundness (logical consistency, evidence–claim alignment, terminology accuracy) or writing quality and language polish — or both equally? The answer materially changes whether R1.2/R1.3 or R1.6 become load-bearing.
